title: Task Manifest Directory in Hadoop MapReduce
description: The 'lib/output/committer/manifest/files' directory in Hadoop MapReduce houses classes crucial for task manifest generation and management. The 'DirEntry' and 'FileEntry' classes represent directory and file entries respectively, encapsulating metadata for task commit. 'DirEntry' defines a directory entry, while 'FileEntry' represents a file entry, both adhering to Hadoop's Writable interface for serialization. 'ManifestSuccessData' class stores job execution summary data, formatted for compatibility with Amazon S3A committer. 'TaskManifest' class represents a task's file manifest, storing metadata about files created during a task attempt, facilitating task commit and data management. These components collaborate to ensure accurate tracking and management of files produced by MapReduce tasks. The directory employs a combination of object-oriented design, serialization for data persistence, and adherence to Hadoop's data processing pipeline standards, exemplifying a well-structured, maintainable, and efficient architectural pattern.

The 'DirEntry' and 'FileEntry' classes represent directory and file entries respectively, encapsulating metadata for task commit. 'DirEntry' defines a directory entry, while 'FileEntry' represents a file entry, both adhering to Hadoop's Writable interface for serialization. 'ManifestSuccessData' class stores job execution summary data, formatted for compatibility with Amazon S3A committer. 'TaskManifest' class represents a task's file manifest, storing metadata about files created during a task attempt, facilitating task commit and data management. These components collaborate to ensure accurate tracking and management of files produced by MapReduce tasks. The directory employs a combination of object-oriented design, serialization for data persistence, and adherence to Hadoop's data processing pipeline standards, exemplifying a well-structured, maintainable, and efficient architectural pattern.
